  • Hide All Code

11_bits_and_pieces

Gulnara Tagridzhanova

04/04/2026

Summary: small things that came up in revisions, too small to warrant their own report, too disjointed to make it worthwhile integrting them in existing reports

1. Non-Tangerine tyrRs in Xanthoria genomes

  • Got this suggestion: “im curious about these other YRs: are orthologous YRs located at the same genomic locus in different genomes? How many are located near the ends of contigs? I think this information will help readers understand why only 2/20 YRs were found associated with a full-length Starship. How likely is it that more interesting starships will be found in Xanthoria if you sequence more genomes?”
  • Let’s see what I can find without burying myself completely

Non-Tangerine YRs from Xanthoria in likely HGT clades

  • Only sequence from (any) Xanthoria is xanthoria.aureola.1_tyr4090 in Clade C

Position of YRs in the assemblies

  • To visualize where they are, need to do make a bed file intersecting the YR bed with the whole genome bed files with windows of 10,000 bp (the latter was preppred in 02_annotate)
#load bedtools v2.31.0
source package b0ed0698-358b-4c9b-9d21-603ea8d6e478

#let's remake bed files with windows of 10,000 bp, because the existing ones are made with the contig names that will not be consistent with the YR file
bedtools makewindows -g  analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/genomes/Xanthoria_parietina_GTX0501.scaffolds.fa.fai   -w 10000 > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/GTX0501.bed

bedtools makewindows -g  analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/genomes/Xanthoria_calcicola_GTX0491.scaffolds.fa.fai   -w 10000 > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/GTX0491.bed

bedtools makewindows -g  analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/genomes/Xanthoria_parietina_SAMEA111342678.scaffolds.fa.fai  -w 10000 > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/ASG.bed

bedtools makewindows -g  analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/genomes/Xanthoria_parietina_XpEllen.scaffolds.fa.fai  -w 10000 > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/XpEllen.bed

##GTX0501
bedtools coverage -a analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/GTX0501.bed -b analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/geneFinder/manyxanthoria.tyr.bed > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/GTX0501_tyr_bedtools.txt

##GTX0491
bedtools coverage -a  analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/GTX0491.bed -b analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/geneFinder/manyxanthoria.tyr.bed > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/GTX0491_tyr_bedtools.txt

#XpEllen
bedtools coverage -a analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/XpEllen.bed -b analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/geneFinder/manyxanthoria.tyr.bed > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/XpEllen_tyr_bedtools.txt

#ASG
bedtools coverage -a analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/ASG.bed -b analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/geneFinder/manyxanthoria.tyr.bed > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/ASG_tyr_bedtools.txt
  • Now let’s visualize them one-by-one. GTX501 does show that most are close to a contig end
plot_tyr<-function(file){
  bed_tyr<-read.delim2(file,header=F)[,c(1,2,7)]
colnames(bed_tyr)<-c("contig","window_start","tyr")
bed_tyr$tyr<-as.numeric(bed_tyr$tyr)
bed_tyr<-bed_tyr %>% mutate(tyr_binary=if_else(tyr>0,1,0))

ggplot(bed_tyr,aes(y=fct_reorder(contig,window_start),x=window_start))+
    geom_tile(aes(height=0.1),fill="black")+
  geom_tile(aes(fill=tyr_binary,alpha=tyr_binary))+
  xlab("")+ylab("")+
  scale_alpha_continuous(range=c(0,1))+
  scale_fill_gradient(low="#f0f0f0",high="#6402bf")+
   scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,1000000,2000000,3000000,4000000),
                     labels = c("0","1 Mbp","2 Mbp","3 Mbp","4 Mbp"))+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank())}

plot_tyr("../analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/GTX0501_tyr_bedtools.txt")

  • GTX491 has fewer just in general, although all the contigs that contain captains have telomeric repeats on both ends
plot_tyr("../analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/GTX0491_tyr_bedtools.txt")

  • ASG has two captains side-by-side on contig 35
plot_tyr("../analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/ASG_tyr_bedtools.txt")

  • XpEllen has two on captian on the end of contig 8
plot_tyr("../analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/XpEllen_tyr_bedtools.txt")

  • The captains from the same navis: are they in the same position in the genomes?
    • In addition to Tangerine (navis0002), only two more naves are represented in >1 genome, navis0001 and navis0003
    • navis0001, phoenixFam: on all three X.parietina the placement is consistent (scaffold_5 in XpEllen, OZ2100_32 in ASG, and XpGTX0501_12 are all in the same block. GTX501 has two sequences in this navis, and the second is in XpGTX0501_4. is this again duplication caused by fragmentation?). However, in X.calcicola the sequence is not at XcGTX0491_5, as would be expected, but at XcGTX0491_15.
    • navis0003, galacticaFam: a bit all over the place. It’s in all three X.parietina but is missing in X.calcicola. It’s on the very end of contigs in two genomes (XpGTX0501_37 and scaffold_8), and not far from end on OZ2100_43. XpGTX0501_37 and scaffold_8 form an ortho block 50-60 kb, based on 17 genes. But OZ2100_43 is matching with scaffold_1!
    • navis0004 has two sequences, but both are only in GTX0501. This is probably another case on Starship related assembly break and consequent gene duplication, same as we saw with Tangerine
    • navis0005-10 have one sequence each
    • sequences from navis0008 and navis0009 sit next to each other on one contig. They both are within the third element that Starfish found, OZ2100_s00003. This element was deemed false positive, since it had an empty site >40bp, was only 17 kb, and lacked auxillary genes
  • Summing up, we have two naves (navis0001 and navis0003) present in multiple genomes which have inconsistent placement, and 7 naves (navis0004-10) that are present in one genome each only. Of the latter set, only two (navis0008 and navis0009) are associated with an element

Do they have any auxillary genes in the vicinity?

  • Let’s examine gene models 100 kb up and down stream of all YRs. Will again use bedtools to get the list of genes
#convert gff3 file that contains all genome annotations to bed
source package /tsl/software/testing/bin/bedops-2.4.28
gff2bed < analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/manyxanthorias.gff3  > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/manyxanthorias.bed

#intersect with window
bedtools window -a analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/geneFinder/manyxanthoria.tyr.bed -b analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/manyxanthorias.bed  -w 100000 > analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/manyxanthoria.tyr.vicinity.bed
  • Let’s load the genes and check their annotations. Will need to check that the genes in the direction faced by the captain (so |-tyr-> |-gene1-> <-gene2-| )
#get the list
list<-read.delim("../analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/manyxanthoria.tyr.vicinity.bed",header=F) %>%
  filter(V16=="gene") 
list<-list %>% mutate(TranscriptID = str_replace(V18,"Name=","")) %>% 
  mutate(TranscriptID = sub(";.*", "", TranscriptID)) %>%
  select(V4,TranscriptID,V9,V10,V11)
colnames(list) <- c("tyrID","TranscriptID","Sequence","Gene_Start","Gene_End")
list$tyrID <- str_replace(list$tyrID,"OZ2100","XANPAOZ2100")
list$tyrID <- str_replace(list$tyrID,"scaffold","XANPARI20")
list$TranscriptID <- str_replace(list$TranscriptID,"XANPAEllen","XANPARI20")
list$tyrID <- str_replace(list$tyrID,"XcGTX0491","XANCAGTX0491")
list$tyrID <- str_replace(list$tyrID,"XpGTX0501","XANPAGTX0501")


#get annotations for InterPro
ann_GTX0501 <- read.delim("../analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/ann/Xanthoria_parietina_GTX0501.annotations.txt")
ann_GTX0491 <- read.delim("../analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/ann/Xanthoria_calcicola_GTX0491.annotations.txt") %>% mutate(TranscriptID = str_replace(TranscriptID,"FUN","XANCAGTX0491"))
ann_ASG <-read.delim("../analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/ann/Xanthoria_parietina_SAMEA111342678.annotations.txt") %>% mutate(TranscriptID = str_replace(TranscriptID,"FUN","XANPAOZ2100"))
ann_XEL <-read.delim("../analysis_and_temp_files/03_starfish/ann/Xanthoria_parietina_SAMEA115359166.annotations.txt") %>% mutate(TranscriptID = str_replace(TranscriptID,"FUN","XANPARI20"))

ann<-rbind(ann_GTX0501, ann_GTX0491 ,ann_ASG,ann_XEL) %>% 
  select(TranscriptID,InterPro)

#get info on the YRs and naves
tyr <- read.delim("../results/captains.txt") %>% dplyr::rename(tyr_Start=Start,tyr_End=End)  %>% 
  select(geneID, navis,family_hit,tyr_Start,tyr_End,Strand)


#combine all together
vicinity<- list %>% left_join(tyr,by=c("tyrID"="geneID")) %>%
  mutate(TranscriptID = paste0(TranscriptID,"-T1")) %>%
  left_join(ann) 

#remove all genes in the wrong position compared to the captain
vicinity <- vicinity %>% filter((Strand=="+" & Gene_Start>tyr_Start) | (Strand=="-" & Gene_Start<tyr_Start))

#calculate the distance between the gene and the YR (shortest distance between ends of each gene)
vicinity <- vicinity %>% mutate(Distance1 = abs(Gene_Start-tyr_Start),
                                Distance2 = abs(Gene_Start-tyr_End),
                                Distance3 = abs(Gene_Start-tyr_Start),
                                Distance4 = abs(Gene_End-tyr_End)) %>%
  mutate(Distance = pmap(across(starts_with("Distance")),min)) %>%
  select(TranscriptID,Sequence,Gene_Start,Gene_End,tyrID,navis,family_hit,tyr_Start,tyr_End,Distance,InterPro)
vicinity$Distance <- vicinity$Distance %>% as.integer
#save
write.table(vicinity,"../analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/genes_in_vicinity_tyr.txt",sep="\t",quote = F, row.names = F)
  • Do any of them have auxillary gene annotations?
    • screened InterPro annotations
    • to identify NLRs, selected proteins that have either NACHT or NB-Arc domain and one of the three repeat domains (Ankyrin, WD40, or TPR)
vicinity_filter<-vicinity %>% mutate(Auxillary_type = case_when(grepl("IPR022198",InterPro) ~ "DUF3723",
      grepl("IPR002641",InterPro) ~ "Patatin-like phospholipase",
  grepl("IPR007111",InterPro) & (grepl("IPR002110",InterPro) | grepl("IPR001680",InterPro) | grepl("IPR019734",InterPro)) ~ "NLR", 
  grepl("IPR002182",InterPro) & (grepl("IPR002110",InterPro) | grepl("IPR001680",InterPro) | grepl("IPR019734",InterPro)) ~ "NLR",
     grepl("IPR013121",InterPro) ~ "Ferric reductase",
  T ~ "")) %>% filter(Auxillary_type != "") %>%
  select(TranscriptID,Sequence,Gene_Start,Gene_End,Auxillary_type,tyrID,navis,family_hit,tyr_Start,tyr_End,Distance,InterPro) %>%
  arrange(navis) 


write.table(vicinity_filter,"../analysis_and_temp_files/11_bits_and_pieces/auxillary_genes_in_vicinity_tyr.txt",sep="\t",quote = F, row.names = F)

vicinity_filter %>% kable(format = "html", col.names = colnames(vicinity_filter)) %>% 
  kable_styling() %>%
  kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "300px")
TranscriptID Sequence Gene_Start Gene_End Auxillary_type tyrID navis family_hit tyr_Start tyr_End Distance InterPro

  • Incorporated these results in the supplementary table listing all tyrRs from Xanthoria
